CheckTitle: AI Agent Frameworks Must Be Scanned for CVEs
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Supply Chain Security**. Validates that agent frameworks such as LangChain and LlamaIndex are continuously scanned for known CVEs. Unpatched framework vulnerabilities are a primary vector for supply chain attacks against AI agents.
Risk: AI agent frameworks are complex, rapidly evolving codebases with frequent security disclosures. An unscanned framework may contain critical RCE, SSRF, or prompt-injection vulnerabilities that attackers can exploit to compromise the entire agent.

Code.Other:
1. Integrate a dependency scanning tool (Dependabot, Snyk, pip-audit) into the CI/CD pipeline.
2. Configure automated alerts for new CVEs affecting pinned framework versions.
3. Establish an SLA to apply security patches within 14 days of disclosure.
4. Pin framework versions and update only after scanning.

Recommendation.Text: Enable continuous CVE scanning for all agent frameworks using a tool such as pip-audit, Snyk, or GitHub Dependabot. Automate alerts and enforce patching SLAs.

Notes: ASPM-068 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
